label: Small Compline (Mikron Apodeipnon)
description: The Byzantine Night Office. Small Compline (Mikron Apodeipnon) is the shorter form of Compline in the Byzantine rite, appointed for ordinary weekdays and seasons outside Great Lent.

sections.label: Opening
sections.notes: Small Compline opens with the Usual Beginning, as do all Byzantine offices.

items.text: The Priest blesses: Blessed is our God, always, now and ever and unto ages of ages.

items.label: The Usual Beginning
items.note: Trisagion prayers sequence. Resolved from compline-fixed.json by HorologionEngine.

sections.label: Fixed Psalmody
sections.notes: Small Compline appoints three fixed psalms: 50, 69, and 142 (LXX). These are invariable on all ordinary days.

items.label: Psalm 50 (LXX) — Have mercy on me, O God
Psalm 69 (LXX) — O God, come to my assistance
Psalm 142 (LXX) — Hear my prayer, O Lord

sections.label: Doxology and Creed
sections.notes: The Lesser Doxology, followed by the Nicene-Constantinopolitan Creed.

sections.label: Trisagion Prayers
sections.notes: The Trisagion prayer sequence precedes the troparia.

sections.label: Troparia
sections.notes: The dismissal troparion of the day is sung. The fixed Theotokion of Compline follows.

items.label: Troparion of the Day
items.note: Variable troparion: resolved via Menaion resolver or weekday-theme rubric by HorologionEngine. Same infrastructure as Vespers troparion slot.

items.label: Theotokion of Compline
items.note: Fixed invariable Theotokion of Small Compline. Resolved from compline-fixed.json by HorologionEngine.

sections.label: Prayer of St. Basil and Dismissal
sections.notes: The Prayer of St. Basil the Great is said at Compline. The office closes with the standard dismissal.

items.label: Prayer of St. Basil the Great

items.text:
Priest: Glory to Thee, O Christ our God, our hope, glory to Thee.
Choir: Glory to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit, both now and ever, and unto ages of ages. Amen. Lord, have mercy. (×3) Father, bless.
Priest: [Dismissal according to the day and feast]
